records.ety: [L. incidens, -entis, p. pr. & of incidere to fall into or upon; pref. in- in, on + cadere to fall: cf. F. incident. See Cadence.]

defs: Falling or striking upon, as a ray of light upon a reflecting surface.
Coming or happening accidentally; not in the usual course of things; not in connection with the main design; not according to expectation; casual; fortuitous.
quotes: As the ordinary course of common affairs is disposed of by general laws, so likewise men's rarer incident necessities and utilities should be with special equity considered.

defs: Liable to happen; apt to occur; befalling; hence, naturally happening or appertaining.
quotes: All chances incident to man's frail life.
The studies incident to his profession.

defs: Dependent upon, or appertaining to, another thing, called the principal.
That which falls out or takes place; an event; casualty; occurrence.
That which happens aside from the main design; an accidental or subordinate action or event.
quotes: No person, no incident, in a play but must be of use to carry on the main design.

defs: Something appertaining to, passing with, or depending on, another, called the principal.
